summary: All seven findings are resolved. P1-LockManagerProtocols-c3f: configure(settings:) is a defaulted protocol extension (no-op), never a required method, so all existing conformers compile without changes. P1-LockOverlayController-a7d: cursor hide/show is fully mode-gated — present() guards CGDisplayHideCursor/NSCursor.hide() behind trackpadMode != .free, sets cursorHidden = true, and dismiss() balances it via the flag. P2-SettingsWindowController-b2e: isReleasedWhenClosed = false is set, showOrFocus() is the single entry point, and AppDelegate holds the strong reference; windowWillClose sets window = nil for correct lifecycle. P2-LockOverlayController-f91: HUD panels use .statusBar window level, not .screenSaver. P2-LockManagerProtocols-d88: FakeEventTapController and FakeEnabledTapController both match the install(trackpadFree:) signature required by the EventTapControlling protocol. P3-QuickPickMenuViewModel-e4c: fixedPresets is a public static constant, allowing tests (QuickPickMenuTests) to reference it directly rather than hardcoding literals. P3-LockOverlayController-77a: screensChanged re-hides cursor only when the cursorHidden flag is already true, preventing double-hide on display hotplug events.
